message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine (Rancho Mirage)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp ~999 reviews + 1,162 photos; TripAdvisor ~350 reviews; Google Maps cited as 300+ positive reviews (combined ~1,500+ across platforms)
- Sentiment: mostly positive (4.3/5 on TripAdvisor) with mixed recent signals
- Longevity: Opened 2012 by father-son team Mark and Drew Davis; ~14 years operating. Late-2024 ownership/management change referenced in recent reviews [UNVERIFIED specifics of new owner identity]
- Credentials: ["TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice (referenced in TripAdvisor profile)","TripAdvisor #5-#7 of ~68-80 Rancho Mirage restaurants","Featured in Palm Springs Life (2013 chef profile)","Featured in Coachella Magazine, Locale Magazine, Coachella Valley Weekly","Gayot listing","Desert Business Association member","Chef Drew Davis trained under James Beard-winning chefs Jonathan Eisman and Mark Militello in Miami; CIA alum; former Exec Sous-Chef at Nick & Toni's (East Hampton); former private chef to Steven Spielberg"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces strongly for: 'best Spanish restaurant Coachella Valley/Palm Springs', 'tapas restaurant near Palm Springs Iberico ham octopus', 'best paella Coachella Valley', 'Spanish restaurants Rancho Mirage'. Surfaces weakly or not at all for: 'date night Rancho Mirage' (loses to The Edge Steakhouse, Wally's Desert Turtle, Shame on the Moon, Willie's Modern Fare), 'best fine dining Rancho Mirage' (omitted from Palm Springs Life and OpenTable curated date-night lists), and generic 'best restaurants Rancho Mirage' top-10 mentions (typically a lower-third mention rather than headline).
- Site structured data: Weak. catalanrestaurants.com is built on GoDaddy Website Builder. NO schema.org / JSON-LD / Restaurant markup detected. Street address is not in visible body content (phone only). Hours are presented as plain text. Menu pages exist but are not semantically marked. Social links to Facebook/Instagram/Yelp present; no OpenTable widget embedded; no online ordering. A second domain (catalanrestaurant.com) hosts an About page, suggesting possible domain split/duplication post-ownership-change.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.content:
# Analysis of Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine Web Content

## Key Findings:

**Schema/Structured Data:** No schema.org JSON-LD or microdata markup detected in the provided HTML source.

**Street Address:** Not visible in the provided content. Location is referenced only as "rancho Mirage" within descriptive text.

**Hours of Operation:** Bar happy hour listed as "5 to 7" pm with a separate section noting "5pm to 7pm" for tapas availability Sunday through Thursday. Full operational hours not specified.

**Online Ordering/OpenTable:** No OpenTable widget or online ordering system mentioned.

**Social Media Links:** Present - Facebook, Instagram, and Yelp profiles are linked in the footer area.

**Owner Information:** No mention of Mark and Drew Davis or any specific ownership names in this content.

**Opening Year:** Not provided.

## Notable Details:

The restaurant describes itself as "a locally owned family run restaurant" offering Spanish, Italian, French, and Greek Mediterranean cuisine, with signature items including paella and charred Spanish octopus. The establishment features "The Boulevard Bar" with house-made sangria and Spanish cocktails.

**Contact:** Phone number (760) 770-9508 is the primary reservation method listed.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Catalan Mediterranean Cuisine:

## Restaurant Overview
Catalan Mediterranean Restaurant is located in the heart of Rancho Mirage and serves a variety of Spanish-infused dishes in a rustic yet modern setting.

## Opening and History
This family owned and operated business has been providing the Desert culinary scene with one of the finest Tapas & Paella experience since 2012. The restaurant is located in the building where Bangkok Five once was on Highway 111.

## Chef Drew Davis
Chef Drew Davis creates seasonal dishes for guests using only the freshest of ingredients. Before opening Catalan, in the early 2000s, he worked as the executive sous chef for Nick and Toni's in the Hamptons, creating dishes for celebrities like Mick Jagger, the Clintons, Tom Hanks, and Billy Joel. Prior to opening the restaurant, Drew had been working in Los Angeles as a personal chef for high profile celebrities.

## Ownership and Philosophy
Mark Davis, the patriarch of the Davis family, was living his retirement out in the Coachella Valley after a successful career in the shipping industry. The Mediterranean cuisine created has captured awards including a 2012 Diner's Choice nod from OpenTable.com.

I've completed the web search for "Catalan" restaurant in Rancho Mirage with Yelp reviews, tapas, and Spanish cuisine. Here's what I found:

## Overview
Catalan is a restaurant in Rancho Mirage that serves delicious and authentic Mediterranean cuisine, specializing in Spanish cuisine including tapas, paella, and sangria.

## Location & Hours
The restaurant is located at 70026 Hwy 111 in Rancho Mirage, CA 92270, and is open Monday through Saturday from 5:00 pm to 9:00 pm, and Sunday from 5:00 pm to 8:00 pm.

## Menu & Specialties
Their Paella mixta and Spanish Tapas are exclusive experiences in the desert, and they feature charred deep water octopus and 3 month house made lemoncello. The deep-water octopus is described as "tender" and "perfectly charred," while the paella is praised as "really flavorful" and "a must-try".

## Dining Experience
The restaurant features a beautiful atrium for dining during cooler months, accompanied by live Spanish guitar featuring flamenco and classic rock music. Live acoustic Spanish guitar is featured every Thursday through Saturday at 6PM during season (November through May).

## Happy Hour
Happy hour is available from 5 pm to 7 pm with a tapas menu only available at the bar.

## Reviews Summary
Customer reviews are generally positive, with diners noting the food is outstanding and authentic, describing it as "maybe the best Spanish food in the valley," with special praise for the tapas and charcuterie, though it's on the pricier side.

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about Chef Drew Davis and his connections to the culinary world:

Drew Davis is the owner of Catalan restaurant, which he launched with his father Mark Davis. Davis attended the Culinary Institute of America, and he has worked under James Beard awarded chefs in Florida and the Midwest.

More specifically, Drew worked at the Culinary Institute of America and worked in Florida and the Mid-West. Moving to Miami in 2004, Drew further worked under Top chefs Jonathan Eisman of Pacific Time, which directly connects to one of the names you mentioned in your search query.

Additionally, Drew was part of James Beard award winning Chefs Michael Smith and Debbie Golds' new Kansas City venture 40 Sardines, where he helped them open in 2001.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best fine dining restaurants in Rancho Mirage for a date night:

## Top Recommendations

**The Edge Steakhouse** at The Ritz-Carlton
Created by award-winning Chef Darryl Boyd, this elegant and sleek steak house serves up only the finest cuts of American Wagyu, all of which are dry-aged in house and served with a delectable array of starters, sides and signature cocktails. For those looking to indulge and impress during their next date night, The Edge Steakhouse is a must-visit.

**Wally's Desert Turtle**
Founded in 1978 by acclaimed restaurateur Wally Botello, Wally's Desert Turtle is a bona fide piece of Rancho Mirage history, revered as the origin of fine dining in the desert, this elevated destination offers up a five-star experience. With everything from the stellar menu crafted by French food extraordinaire Jean-Louis Jalouneix to its elegant, classical interior meticulously designed to create a remarkably unique yet timeless feel, dining at Wally's is a feast for all the senses.

**Willie's Modern Fare**
Restaurateur Willie Rhine seamlessly pairs five-star hospitality with the elegance of fine dining, offering classic cuisine with a modern twist, complemented by inventive cocktails and a thoughtfully curated wine list.

**Si Bon Belgian Bistro**
For more than a decade, Philippe Caupain has drawn regulars to Si Bon with Belgian favorites that range from crêpes and mussels to waffles topped with escargot, with a shaded patio, a dining room styled like a European café, and a sleek bar where locals gather over wine and cocktails.

All of these restaurants offer romantic atmospheres and exceptional cuisine perfect for a special date night in Rancho Mirage.

Based on the search results, I found information about Catalan Mediterranean Restaurant in Rancho Mirage:

Catalan is a locally owned family run restaurant located in the heart of Rancho Mirage. The restaurant has been serving Mediterranean Cuisine since 2013.

**Location & Contact:**
The restaurant is located at 70026 Highway 111 between Frank Sinatra and Country Club in Rancho Mirage, CA 92270. The phone number is (760) 770-9508, and the website is catalanrestaurants.com.

**Menu & Cuisine:**
They blend authentic flavors of Spain, Italy, France and Greece. Their Spanish-inspired menu includes traditional specialties such as Paella and charred Spanish octopus, and they carry many imported meats for charcuterie boards like Iberico Ham de Bellota. They also offer a bar with an extensive menu including house-made sangria and Spanish-inspired cocktails.

**Hours:**
According to their Yelp listing, they're open Monday through Saturday from 5:00 PM to 9:00 PM, and Sunday from 5:00 PM to 8:00 PM.

content.text: I have enough verification. Note: one search result says "since 2013" while others consistently say 2012; the 2012 figure is corroborated by the Palm Springs Life 2013 chef profile and multiple secondary sources.

confirms: Yelp review volume: independently confirmed 999 reviews and 1,162 photos on Yelp listing (70026 Hwy 111)
TripAdvisor ranking #5 of 80 Rancho Mirage restaurants (claim said #5-#7 of ~68-80; verified at #5 of 80)
TripAdvisor Travelers' Choice award is currently displayed on the TripAdvisor profile
Longevity: Opened 2012 by father-son Mark and Drew Davis confirmed across multiple secondary sources (Locale Magazine, Coachella Magazine, desertluxuryproperties)
Chef Drew Davis credentials: CIA alum confirmed; trained under Jonathan Eisman (Pacific Time, Miami) confirmed; worked at Nick & Toni's in the Hamptons confirmed
Featured in Palm Springs Life (2013 chef profile), Coachella Magazine, Locale Magazine confirmed

Site structured data weakness: independent fetch of catalanrestaurants.com confirms NO JSON-LD/schema.org markup, no visible street address (phone-only contact), social links to Facebook/Instagram/Yelp present, no OpenTable widget on site, no online ordering

Visibility gap: Catalan does NOT appear in Palm Springs Life '6 Rancho Mirage Restaurants Worth a Reservation' or OpenTable curated date-night/fine-dining top lists; competitors named (The Edge Steakhouse, Wally's Desert Turtle, Willie's Modern Fare) verified as the dominant date-night/fine-dining recommendations
Strong surfacing for Spanish/tapas/paella queries confirmed — Catalan is the top result for 'best Spanish restaurants Rancho Mirage'
contradicts: Claim of 'late-2024 ownership/management change' — I could not find ANY independent corroboration of an ownership change. Multiple recent (2026) sources still describe Catalan as Davis family-run with Drew Davis as chef-owner. The 'Catalan Rancho Mirage' Facebook page (id 61567913220411) created separately from the original CatalanRestaurant FB page may indicate a marketing/operational split but is not proof of ownership change. This claim is more likely inaccurate than accurate.

Claim that a second domain 'catalanrestaurant.com hosts an About page' — the domain returned a TLS certificate hostname mismatch error (ERR_TLS_CERT_ALTNAME_INVALID), suggesting it is NOT a properly configured live site hosting an About page. The active site is catalanrestaurants.com (plural).
flagged_as_unverified: Google Maps '300+ positive reviews' — not independently verified (Google review count not surfaced in searches)
Mark Militello specifically as Drew Davis's mentor — Jonathan Eisman is well-corroborated, but Militello is named only in the original claim; not independently confirmed

Steven Spielberg as a private-chef client — secondary sources mention generic 'high-profile celebrities' and name Mick Jagger, Clintons, Tom Hanks, Billy Joel (at Nick & Toni's) but Spielberg specifically not confirmed
Mixed-recent-signals sentiment shift tied to ownership change — without confirming the ownership change itself, the framing is unsupported
Exact identity of any new owner (already self-flagged as UNVERIFIED in claim, confirmed unverifiable here)
input.notes: Core visibility picture is solidly verified: high review volume on Yelp/TripAdvisor, Travelers' Choice + #5/80 TripAdvisor rank, weak schema/structured data on the GoDaddy-built site, and clear absence from curated date-night/fine-dining lists that favor The Edge, Wally's, and Willie's. The two material problems with the assessment: (1) the alleged 2024 ownership change has zero independent corroboration and should be downgraded or removed, and (2) the 'second domain catalanrestaurant.com hosts an About page' claim appears wrong — the domain has a TLS cert mismatch and is not serving content. Recommend revising visibility-gap score commentary to drop unverified ownership-change narrative; convertibility tier 'convertible' remains valid because the schema-zero GoDaddy site is the genuine fixable weakness.
